element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Random projectile throws from screen edges (JP version only)
First two mini-bosses require head-targeting in JP version
White and black stars (US version); 'abeshi' and 'hidebu' variants (JP version)

typical_relief_triggers: Stage completion with NPC scenes (JP version)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Wave-based combat pacing with mini-boss punctuation; continuous pressure alternating with power-up relief windows

difficulty_curve.early_game: Foundational combat mechanics introduction with basic enemy patterns
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Mini-boss encounters with targeting requirements (JP version harder); increased enemy density
notable_spikes: First two mini-boss fights (JP version requires head-targeting)
Stage 4 difficulty spike (continue point in JP version; full stage restart in US version)

notable_level_patterns.description: Game organized into sequential stages with mini-boss punctuation; beat-em-up flow with wave-based enemy encounters
JP version continues from Stage 4 (global checkpoint); US version continues from current stage (local checkpoint). Affects pacing and difficulty perception
Stage completion features NPC appearances creating narrative punctuation and pacing breaks; removed in US version

jp_difficulty_factors: Mini-boss head-targeting requirement for first two bosses
us_version_changes: Kenshiro color changed black to blue
Power-up visual redesign (stars instead of JP variants)
Ending credit bug (noise instead of music)

data_quality_notes: T3 qualitative data. Source provides mechanical and regional differences but lacks specific level layout documentation, exact stage count, timing data, and spatial structure details. Element introduction inferred from game type (beat-em-up) and available mechanical descriptions.
